title: Minnesota Sues Federal Government for Withholding Evidence in Three ICE Shootings
excerpt: Minnesota Attorney General Keith Ellison and Hennepin County Attorney Mary Moriarty filed a federal lawsuit accusing the DOJ and DHS of refusing to share evidence in the fatal shootings of Renee Good and Alex Pretti and the non-fatal shooting of Julio Sosa-Celis, including blocking state investigators from accessing scenes and evidence.
content:
On March 24, 2026, Minnesota Attorney General Keith Ellison, Hennepin County Attorney Mary Moriarty, and the superintendent of the Minnesota Bureau of Criminal Apprehension filed a federal lawsuit in D.C. district court, accusing the Department of Justice and Department of Homeland Security of refusing to cooperate with state investigations into all three Minneapolis shootings by federal officers.

The lawsuit alleged that in the Good and Pretti cases, federal agents initially agreed to work jointly with state investigators, then took unilateral control of all evidence. At the Pretti shooting scene, federal agents physically blocked state investigators from accessing it even after they obtained a warrant.

  • Evidence the state said it could not access included Alex Pretti's cell phone, Renee Good's car (described as shrink-wrapped in an FBI warehouse and never examined), and agent Jonathan Ross's complete training and personnel files.
  • A federal judge separately ordered the government to turn over Ross's unredacted training records, personnel files, and ICE use-of-force policies for an in-camera review — directly implicating training documentation as central evidence in the shooting probe.
  • State officials noted that in all three shootings, federal authorities quickly labeled victims as threats, despite video evidence contradicting those accounts.
  • DHS confirmed two officers in the Sosa-Celis case "appeared to have made untruthful statements" and placed them on administrative leave, but did not answer questions about discipline for the agents who killed Pretti and Good.
mediaPlaceholder: Federal lawsuit filing by Minnesota AG seeking access to shooting evidence and agent training records
